channel.title: NPR Topics: World
channel.description: NPR world news, international art and culture, world business and financial markets, world economy, and global trends in health, science and technology. Subscribe to the World Story of the Day podcast and RSS feed.
channel.copyright: Copyright 2024 NPR - For Personal Use Only

item.title: U.S. and Iran escalate strikes across Mideast
item.description: The United States and Iran exchanged strikes aimed at infrastructure and military targets on Saturday as their battle over the Strait of Hormuz intensified.
item.content:encoded:
The United States and Iran exchanged strikes aimed at infrastructure and military targets on Saturday as their battle over the Strait of Hormuz intensified.

(Image credit: Razieh Poudat/AP Photo)
item.dc:creator: The Associated Press

item.title: China signals possible return of U.S. trade privileges for Hong Kong
item.description: The decision comes two months after President Donald Trump met with Xi Jinping, potentially warming ties ahead of Xi's expected U.S. visit.
item.content:encoded:
The decision comes two months after President Donald Trump met with Xi Jinping, potentially warming ties ahead of Xi's expected U.S. visit.

(Image credit: Mark Schiefelbein)
item.dc:creator: The Associated Press

item.title: The U.S.-Iran battle over the Strait of Hormuz raises risks for global waterways
item.description: Recognizing Iran's control over the Strait of Hormuz could set a dangerous precedent, with other countries attempting to claim important waterways, analysts say.
item.content:encoded:
Recognizing Iran's control over the Strait of Hormuz could set a dangerous precedent, with other countries attempting to claim important waterways, analysts say.

(Image credit: AFP via Getty Images)
item.dc:creator: Jackie Northam

item.title: Pakistani forces kill 24 militants in border raids near Afghanistan
item.description: The military said Friday it used intelligence sources to target militants over the previous 24 hours. The operations were in response to attacks by militants earlier in the week.
item.content:encoded:
The military said Friday it used intelligence sources to target militants over the previous 24 hours. The operations were in response to attacks by militants earlier in the week.

(Image credit: AP)
item.dc:creator: The Associated Press

item.title: U.S. strikes bridges in Iran; Tehran targets U.S. bases in the Gulf
item.description: The U.S. and Iran expanded their targets in the latest round of strikes on Friday, as fighting over the control of the Strait of Hormuz reignites fears of an all-out war.
item.content:encoded:
The U.S. and Iran expanded their targets in the latest round of strikes on Friday, as fighting over the control of the Strait of Hormuz reignites fears of an all-out war.

(Image credit: AFP via Getty Images)
item.dc:creator: NPR Staff

item.title: China's Xi calls for step up of global effort in AI, as US curbs squeeze China's tech access
item.description: American-led restrictions have blocked China from accessing some of the world's most advanced technologies, spurring China's efforts to build its own know-how and intensifying the tech race between the world's two biggest economies.
item.content:encoded:
American-led restrictions have blocked China from accessing some of the world's most advanced technologies, spurring China's efforts to build its own know-how and intensifying the tech race between the world's two biggest economies.

(Image credit: Ng Han Guan)
item.dc:creator: The Associated Press

item.title: Rubio convenes countries on left-wing political violence
item.description: Secretary of State Marco Rubio on Thursday convened leaders from more than 60 countries to take part in the Trump administration's latest effort to quell what it calls "left wing" political terrorism.
item.content:encoded:
Secretary of State Marco Rubio on Thursday convened leaders from more than 60 countries to take part in the Trump administration's latest effort to quell what it calls "left wing" political terrorism.

(Image credit: Julia Demaree Nikhinson)
item.dc:creator: The Associated Press

item.title: Japan revises law on royals, keeps blocking women from throne
item.description: A popular princess drives support for having a female Japanese Emperor. But the country's first female prime minister opposes it.
item.content:encoded:
A popular princess drives support for having a female Japanese Emperor. But the country's first female prime minister opposes it.

(Image credit: Tomohiro Ohsumi)
item.dc:creator: Anthony Kuhn

item.title: Boris Nadezhdin is a rare Russian politician speaking out against the Ukraine war
item.description: Boris Nadezhdin wants to run for Russia's parliament on a platform that gives voice to the Russians against the war in Ukraine. That's why, he says, the Kremlin is doing everything to stop him.
item.content:encoded: Boris Nadezhdin wants to run for Russia's parliament on a platform that gives voice to the Russians against the war in Ukraine. That's why, he says, the Kremlin is doing everything to stop him.
item.dc:creator: Charles Maynes

item.title: Hong Kong official warns booksellers on security risks after raids
item.description: The police operation marks the third round of arrests targeting independent bookstores in four months.
item.content:encoded:
The police operation marks the third round of arrests targeting independent bookstores in four months.

(Image credit: Kanis Leung)
item.dc:creator: The Associated Press
